sast_review.reason: xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring is used on untrusted user input (profile_xml). This is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. No defusedxml or other protection is applied.
Same vulnerability as line 30. The login_handler function also directly calls ET.fromstring on untrusted profile_xml without any protection.

This is a duplicate of bandit:B314 (same root cause: unsafe XML parsing). The import-level warning is less specific but still valid. Marking as uncertain because the real vulnerability is already captured by B314.

This is an import-level warning about subprocess module usage. The module itself is not inherently insecure; the risk depends on how it is used. The actual command injection risk is captured by bandit:B603.

subprocess.run is called with a user-controlled argument (report_type) without sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary commands via the report_type parameter. The executable path is fixed to /usr/local/bin/generate_report, but the --type argument is unsanitized.

raw.summary: Multiple security issues: unsafe XML parsing (XXE) at two locations, command injection via report_type, and a functional regression (login now requires profile_xml, breaking the spec). SAST findings are all true positives or uncertain.
